2606.17775.title: A Neuromorphic Trigger for Efficient Audio Event Detection
2606.17775.paper_summary: Efficient processing of continuous audio streams remains a key challenge for real-time and resource-constrained systems. This paper introduces a neuromorphic trigger for audio event detection, based on a spiking neural network (SNN) that selectively gates input to downstream models. The proposed trigger acts as a low-cost front-end, identifying salient audio segments and forwarding only these to a more computationally intensive model for tasks such as classification. The trigger is implemented as a lightweight fully connected SNN and evaluated on two representative tasks: Anomalous Sound Detection (ASD) and Sound Event Detection (SED). For ASD, the trigger achieves a one-second segment-based F1 score of 0.97 on a class-agnostic form of the URBAN-SED dataset, demonstrating high reliability in identifying relevant audio regions. For SED, the trigger is combined with the Dang classifier on the DCASE 2017 Challenge Task 2 dataset, showing a potential $42.6\times$ reduction in FLOPs while reducing the lower bound of the event-based error rate from 0.41 to 0.25. These results highlight the potential of neuromorphic triggers as real-time, energy-efficient front-end filters, enabling substantial reductions in computational cost.
2606.17775.paper_summary_zh: 有效處理連續音頻流仍然是實時和資源受限系統的一個主要挑戰。這篇論文介紹了一種基於尖峰神經網絡（SNN）的神經形態觸發器，用於音頻事件檢測，該觸發器選擇性地控制輸入到下游模型的數據。所提出的觸發器作為一個低成本的前端，識別顯著的音頻片段，並僅將這些片段轉發給計算密集型模型以進行分類等任務。該觸發器實現為一個輕量級的全連接SNN，並在兩個代表性任務上進行評估：異常聲音檢測（ASD）和聲音事件檢測（SED）。對於ASD，該觸發器在URBAN-SED數據集的無類別形式上實現了一秒片段基於F1分數的0.97，顯示出在識別相關音頻區域方面的高可靠性。對於SED，該觸發器與Dang分類器在DCASE 2017挑戰任務2數據集上結合，顯示出潛在的$42.6\times$ FLOPs減少，同時將基於事件的錯誤率下限從0.41降低到0.25。這些結果突顯了神經形態觸發器作為實時、節能的前端過濾器的潛力，實現了計算成本的顯著降低。
2606.17775.author: Benjamin Hatton et.al.
2606.17775.authors: Benjamin Hatton, Oliver Rhodes, Luca Peres
